summary.intent: Fix 6 pass-2 Codex review findings in the DateTimePicker component, covering calendar fidelity, day-range clamping, dialog accessibility naming, and spec todo!() placeholder removal.
summary.outcome: All 6 findings resolved: calendar conversion in calendar_props and sync_props, day-range refresh in init/rebuild_date_segments/set_segment_value, aria-label via content_label message, spec todo!() arms filled in. 131 tests pass, all 10 xci-fast gates green, committed and pushed.

repo: CalendarDate::to_calendar exists for converting dates between calendar systems — used in calendar_props and sync_props to maintain fidelity when the calendar prop changes.
IntlBackend::days_in_month provides the correct max-day bound for a given year/month in any calendar system.
DatePicker uses a content_label message key to set the aria-label on its dialog content — mirrors the same pattern as DateField.
Day-range must be refreshed in three places: init, rebuild_date_segments, and set_segment_value to stay consistent across all mutation paths.
Codex runs in passes; pass-2 surfaces findings introduced or missed by pass-1 fixes, including regressions like todo!() placeholders added during the prior fix round.
Snapshot tests capture aria-label changes — adding content_label broke the 'content' snapshot which needed regeneration and acceptance.

code.finding: calendar_props must convert min/max/value dates via to_calendar(ctx.calendar) so they match the active calendar system.
sync_props must detect a calendar change as a structural change and re-derive date_value in the new calendar, not just copy the old value.
refresh_day_range added to Context and called in init (after context construction), rebuild_date_segments, and set_segment_value.
value.get() returns &Option<...> — accessing the inner value requires .as_ref() before pattern matching or unwrapping.
Section §1.7 had todo!() placeholders for segment focus/editing arms — these are a no-deferral violation and must be filled with real implementation bodies before committing.

workflow: Multi-pass Codex review catches regressions introduced by earlier fix rounds — treat each pass as potentially surfacing new issues, not just confirming prior ones.
After accepting a snapshot update, always re-run the full test suite to confirm only the intended change was captured.
xci-fast (10 gates) is the pre-push verification gate; all must be green before committing.
Resolve and reply to Codex threads after pushing the fix, then re-trigger Codex for the next pass.

friction: API socket connection closed unexpectedly mid-session, interrupting work just before the test-writing step — required a manual 'Continue' prompt to resume.
Test compile error: value.get() returning &Option<...> instead of Option<...> caused a fixup round after the initial test addition.
open_items: Codex pass-3 may surface additional findings after the pass-2 fixes are reviewed — threads were replied to and Codex re-triggered but results are pending.
